metadata.summary: Progressive Web App requires Web App Manifest with stable id and maskable icons, Service Worker from origin root, and update notification on controllerchange. Use Workbox for caching strategies.

content.title: PWA Core Architecture
content.summary: A Progressive Web App requires three components: a Web App Manifest (manifest.json with stable id, maskable icons, display: standalone), a Service Worker served from the origin root (not /static/), and a registration module that defers the install prompt and shows an update notification on controllerchange. Use Workbox for caching strategy — NetworkFirst for navigation and API routes, CacheFirst for images and fonts, StaleWhileRevalidate for JS/CSS.

why.p: PWAs close the gap between web and native: offline tolerance, installability, push notifications, and LCP gains from precaching the app shell — without an app store. NetworkFirst with a 3-second timeout prevents the spinner-of-death on flaky networks while keeping data fresh. Workbox handles service worker lifecycle (install, activate, update) so manual cache management is not needed.

list.item: Web app needs offline tolerance, install prompt, push notifications, or "add to home screen."
One codebase targeting desktop, Android, and iOS without an app store.
Caching layer must survive flaky networks (transit, kiosk, captive Wi-Fi).
Content/marketing site needing LCP/INP wins from precaching critical assets.
iOS-first product where push, background sync, and persistent storage limits block the use case.
Hard real-time apps (live trading, video conferencing) — SW cache-coherence headaches, no upside.
Highly regulated UI (banking transactions) where stale cache is a compliance risk.
App requiring deep OS integration (Bluetooth, full camera, contacts) unreachable on iOS PWA.

section.name: service-worker-location
section.p: Service worker scope is tied to its path. Origin-root placement controls the entire app.

rule.statement: Serve sw.js and manifest.json from the origin root (/) with Cache-Control: no-cache. Never serve the service worker from /static/ or any subdirectory.
rule.rationale: Service worker scope is tied to its path. A SW at /static/sw.js can only control resources under /static/. Origin-root placement controls the entire app.

section.name: manifest-stability
section.p: Keep the manifest id field stable across deploys. Changing it forces browsers to treat the PWA as a new app.

rule.statement: Keep the manifest id field stable across deploys. Never change it after the first release.
rule.rationale: Changing id forces browsers to treat the PWA as a new app, breaking existing shortcuts and triggering re-installation prompts.

section.name: icons
section.p: Android crops non-maskable icons into unexpected shapes on home screen. Always use the maskable purpose.

rule.statement: Ship all icon entries with purpose: "maskable any". Never ship icons with only purpose: "any".
rule.rationale: Android crops non-maskable icons into unexpected shapes on home screen. maskable ensures the safe-zone guarantee.

section.name: caching-strategy
section.p: Different asset types need different caching strategies. NetworkFirst with timeout avoids spinner-of-death on flaky networks.

rule.statement: Use NetworkFirst with networkTimeoutSeconds: 3 for /api/* and navigation routes. Use CacheFirst for images and fonts. Use StaleWhileRevalidate for JS/CSS static assets. Never precache personalised HTML or API responses.
rule.rationale: NetworkFirst with timeout avoids spinner-of-death on flaky networks. CacheFirst for immutable assets (images, fonts) reduces redundant fetches.

section.name: update-ux
section.p: Auto-reload discards in-flight form data and violates user expectations. The controllerchange event fires when the new SW takes control.

rule.statement: Show an update prompt on the controllerchange event. Never auto-reload mid-form. Present a user-initiated reload action.
rule.rationale: Auto-reload discards in-flight form data and violates user expectations. The controllerchange event fires when the new SW takes control.

section.name: ios-limitations
section.p: These gaps make offline-first PWAs unreliable for iOS. Agents never surface this in code review; humans must sign off on any iOS PWA claim.

rule.statement: Document iOS Safari PWA limitations before promising any PWA feature to iOS users: storage evicted after ~7 days of non-use, no background sync, no periodic sync, no push on older iOS.
rule.rationale: These gaps make offline-first PWAs unreliable for iOS. Agents never surface this in code review; humans must sign off on any iOS PWA claim.

section.name: service-worker-registration
section.p: Register the service worker once from a single bootstrap module. Do not register inside React effects, as StrictMode double-invokes effects in development.

rule.statement: Register the service worker once from a single bootstrap module (index.html or main.ts). Do not register inside React effects — StrictMode double-invokes effects in development, causing double registration.
rule.rationale: Double registration causes confusing lifecycle events and makes debugging SW updates harder.

example.title: Service worker registration with update prompt

section.name: workbox-caching-strategies
section.p: Workbox provides precaching, routing, and strategy helpers. Register routes for pages, API, images, and fonts with appropriate strategies.

example.title: Minimal Workbox strategy registration

section.name: install-prompt
section.p: The beforeinstallprompt event fires once per session. Store it at module scope or in a singleton hook to avoid losing it on re-mount.

example.title: useInstallPrompt hook for install UI

antipattern.description: LLMs hallucinate Workbox import names across versions (NetworkFirst vs networkFirst, CacheFirst vs cacheFirst).
antipattern.reason: Pin the Workbox major version and feed agent the exact import surface from the pinned package's types.

antipattern.description: SW code that reaches for window or document properties.
antipattern.reason: SW runs in ServiceWorkerGlobalScope, not window. Lint with eslint-plugin-workbox or a SW-specific tsconfig that excludes lib: ["dom"].

antipattern.description: beforeinstallprompt is captured inside a component that re-mounts.
antipattern.reason: The event fires once per session. Re-mounting loses the deferred prompt. Store it at module scope or in a singleton hook.

template.purpose: Full manifest with icons (all sizes), shortcuts, screenshots, stable id.
Workbox SW: precache, NavigationRoute, API/image/font/static strategies, offline fallback.
Playwright smoke test: SW registration + offline navigation + manifest reachability.
